NWS SURVEY CONFIRMS A EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 4.4 MILES NORTHEAST OF KILBOURNE AND TRAVELED NORTHEAST FOR 7 MILES. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 8 MINUTES AND WAS 50 YDS WIDE. THE TORNADO DID TREE DAMAGE AND KNOCKED OVER 6 IRRIGATION SYSTEMS. WINDS ARE ESTIMATED AT 75 MPH.

*** 1 FATAL *** DELAYED REPORT. A MAN TRIED TO DRIVE ACROSS A FLOODED ROAD SOUTHWEST OF SHELBYVILLE ON MARCH 31. HIS CAR WAS PUSHED OFF THE ROAD BY THE CURRENT, AND HE WAS SWEPT AWAY AFTER EXITING THE VEHICLE. 24-HOUR RAINFALL IN THAT AREA RANGED FROM 2.5 TO 3 INCHES.
FOUR TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN, INCLUDING ONE THAT FELL ONTO A HOUSE. PART OF THE ROOF OF A TIN SHED WAS RIPPED OFF, THE GUTTERING OF ANOTHER SHED WAS DAMAGED, AND A SIGN WAS BROKEN.
DELAYED REPORT. SHED BLOWN OVER, APPEARED TO BE A RELATIVE NEW, WELL-CONSTRUCTED STRUCTURE. EXACT LOCATION NOT PROVIDED. REPORT TIME AND LOCATION ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.

DELAYED ENTRY - A BRIEF EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS UP TO 105 MPH IMPACTED THE WESTERN AND SOUTHERN PORTIONS OF BEASON IN EAST CENTRAL LOGAN COUNTY. THE TORNADO DEVELOPED AROUND 5:12 AM ON THE FAR WEST SIDE OF BEASON AND STRUCK A LARGE GRAIN ELEVATOR DAMAGING A SOME LARGE BINS AND EQUIPMENT ON THE FAR WEST END OF CLINTON STREET IN BEASON. THE TORNADO TRACKED SOUTHEAST AROUND 50 MPH AND DAMAGED SOME LARGE TREES AND SOME POWERLINES ON THE WEST END OF COUNTY ROAD 1549 N AND ALSO NEAR INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 2143 E AND COUNTY ROAD 1540 N. SOME ROOF DAMAGE TO A FEW HOUSES WITH MISSING SHINGLES AND ALSO ONE HOUSE HAD A WINDOW BROKEN. TORNADO LENGTH WAS 1.85 MILES. MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 60 YARDS.

EF1 TORNADO ESTIMATED WITH 105 MPH WINDS DAMAGED LARGE GRAIN BINS ON FAR WEST END OF BEASON AND TOOK DOWN SOME LARGE TREES AND BRANCHES NEAR A FEW HOMES WITH SOME ROOF DAMAGE AND A WINDOW DAMAGED. TORNADO THEN TRAVELED NEAR 2 MILES SOUTHEAST OF BEASON OVER CORNFIELDS LIFTING AT 515 AM.
THREE TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND A GAZEBO WAS TOSSED ONTO THE ROOF OF A HOUSE, CAUSING DAMAGE TO THE ROOF.

EF-0 TORNADO WITH 85 MPH WINDS, 30 YARDS WIDE AND 3.4 MILE TRACK LENGTH. TORNADO STARTED 0.5 MILES SW LE ROY AT 12:28 AM CDT, LIFTED 3.25 MILES SE LE ROY AT 12:34 AM CDT. TORNADO KNOCKED POWER POLE INTO A MOBILE HOME AND A TREE INTO ANOTHER MOBILE HOME.
